原文: Let's take a minute to think about the water we use. The human body is 60% water and we need to drink lots of water to be healthy. When we are thirsty we just go to the kitchen and fill a glass with clean water.
翻译: 让我们花一分钟思考一下我们使用的水。人体60%是水，我们需要喝大量的水来保持健康。当我们口渴时，我们只需去厨房，用干净的水装满一杯。

原文: A. We use water indirectly① too. For example, farmers, who produce the food we eat, use water to make the plants grow. When we turn on a light or switch on a TV or a computer we use energy and we need water to produce this energy.
翻译: A. 我们也间接地使用水。例如，生产我们食物的农民用水来使植物生长。当我们打开灯、电视或电脑时，我们使用能源，而我们需要水来生产这种能源。
必背词汇:
① indirectly /ˌɪndəˈrektli/ adv. 间接地
Ⓡ indirect /ˌɪndəˈrekt/ adj. 间接的
Ⓡ direct /dəˈrekt/ adj. 直接的 v. 指导
Ⓡ direction /dəˈrekʃn/ n. 方向；指导
Ⓡ director /dəˈrektə(r)/ n. 导演；主管

原文: The truth is that we are lucky enough to have clean water whenever we want, but this is not the case for many people around the world. E. Did you know that around 750 million people do not have clean water to drink? That's around one in 10 people in the world. If we drink dirty water, we can catch diseases② from the bacteria③ and become ill. Every year over 500,000 children die from diarrhoea (腹泻) from dirty water. That's around 1,400 children every day! Also, in some countries children walk many kilometres every day to get water. D. If children walk many hours a day to get water, they can't go to school. Therefore, they don't have time to learn how to read or write and don't get an education④.
翻译: 事实是，我们足够幸运，可以随时获得干净的水，但这对世界上许多人来说并非如此。E. 你知道吗？大约有7.5亿人没有干净的水喝。这大约占世界人口的十分之一。如果我们喝脏水，我们可能会因细菌感染疾病而生病。每年有超过50万儿童因脏水导致的腹泻而死亡。这大约是每天1400名儿童！此外，在一些国家，儿童每天要走许多公里去取水。D. 如果儿童每天花很多小时去取水，他们就不能上学。因此，他们没有时间学习读写，也无法接受教育。
必背词汇:
② disease /dɪˈziːz/ n. 疾病
Ⓡ ease /iːz/ n. 轻松 v. 减轻
Ⓡ easy /ˈiːzi/ adj. 容易的
Ⓡ easily /ˈiːzəli/ adv. 容易地
③ bacteria /bækˈtɪəriə/ n. 细菌（复数）
Ⓡ bacterium /bækˈtɪəriəm/ n. 细菌（单数）
Ⓡ bacterial /bækˈtɪəriəl/ adj. 细菌的
Ⓡ virus /ˈvaɪrəs/ n. 病毒
④ education /ˌedʒuˈkeɪʃn/ n. 教育
Ⓡ educate /ˈedʒukeɪt/ v. 教育
Ⓡ educational /ˌedʒuˈkeɪʃənl/ adj. 教育的
Ⓡ educator /ˈedʒukeɪtə(r)/ n. 教育者

原文: F. In 1993 the United Nations decided that March 22nd is the World Day for Water. On this day every year, countries around the world hold events to educate people about the problems of dirty water and that clean water is something that everyone should have around the world. At one school in the UK, children between the ages of 10 and 15 walk 6km with six litres of water. G. In this way, they know how it feels to walk a long distance⑤ carrying heavy bottles. People give them money to do this and all the money helps get clean water to as many people as possible around the world.
翻译: F. 1993年，联合国决定3月22日为世界水日。每年的这一天，世界各国举办活动，教育人们关于脏水的问题，以及干净的水是全世界每个人都应该拥有的东西。在英国的一所学校，10到15岁的儿童带着6升水走6公里。G. 通过这种方式，他们了解带着沉重的水瓶走长距离的感觉。人们给他们钱来做这件事，所有的钱都帮助为世界上尽可能多的人提供干净的水。
必背词汇:
⑤ distance /ˈdɪstəns/ n. 距离
Ⓡ distant /ˈdɪstənt/ adj. 遥远的
Ⓡ instance /ˈɪnstəns/ n. 例子
Ⓡ instant /ˈɪnstənt/ adj. 立即的
